title: Spring In War Time
content:

The faint, far scent of bud and leaf,
Oh, how can spring take heart to come
To a world in grief,
Deep grief?

content:

title: Crowned

As we used to walk at night,
Watching our shadows lengthen
Under the gold street-light
When the snow was new and white.

First stars

God

title: The Wine
content:

From the cup of the crescent moon,
And hungrily as men eat bread,

title: The Wind
content:
A wind is blowing over my soul,
I hear it cry the whole night through,
Is there no peace for me on earth
Except with you?

title: Arcturus
content:
Arcturus brings the spring back
As surely now as when
He rose on eastern islands
For Grecian girls and men;

title: The Shrine
content:
There is no lord within my heart,
Left silent as an empty shrine
Where rose and myrtle intertwine,
Within a place apart.

No god is there of carve

title: Redbirds
content:
Redbirds, redbirds,
Long and long ago,
What a honey-call you had
In hills I used to know;

Redbud, buckberry,

title: Did You Never Know
content:
Did you never know, long ago, how much you loved me,
That your love would never lessen and never go?
You were young then, proud and fresh-hearted,

title: Union Square
content:
With the man I love who loves me not,
I walked in the street-lamps' flare;
We watched the world go home that night
In a flood through Union Square.

title: Sea Longing
content:

Somewhere the waves creep cool along the sand,
The ebbing tide forsakes the listless land

title: While I May
content:
Wind and hail and veering rain,
Driven mist that veils the day,
Soul's distress and body's pain,
I would bear you while I may.

title: Two Minds
content:

title: I Would Live In Your Love

content:

title: A Boy
content:

title: May Day
content:

The smell of wet wild earth

title: Sappho II
content:
Oh Litis, little slave, why will you sleep?
These long Egyptian noons bend down your head
Bowed like the yarrow with a yellow bee.

title: Dooryard Roses
content:
I have come the selfsame path
To the selfsame door,
Years have left the roses there
Burning as before

While I watch them in the wind

title: February Twilight
content:
I stood beside a hill
Smooth with new-laid snow,
A single star looked out
From the cold evening glow.

There was no other creature

title: A Little While
content:
A little while when I am gone
My life will live in music after me,
As spun foam lifted and borne on
After the wave is lost in the full sea.

title: Nahant
content:

So earth is bowed, under her weight of splendor,

title: Dusk In Autumn
content:
The moon is like a scimitar,
A little silver scimitar,
A-drifting down the sky.
And near beside it is a star,

title: Evening: New York
content:

Over the ocean of roofs and the tall towers
Where the window-lights, myriads and myriads,

title: Pain
content:

But why for my shimmering body

title: After Parting
content:
Oh, I have sown my love so wide
That he will find it everywhere;
It will awake him in the night,
It will enfold him in the air.

title: Broadway
content:
This is the quiet hour; the theaters
Have gathered in their crowds, and steadily
The million lights blaze on for few to see,

title: New Love And Old
content:
In my heart the old love
Struggled with the new;
It was ghostly waking
All night through.

Dear things, kind things,
That my old love said,

title: The Wanderer
content:
I saw the sunset-colored sands,
The Nile like flowing fire between,
Where Rameses stares forth serene,
And Ammon's heavy temple stands.

title: The Old Maid
content:

The woman I might grow to be;
I felt my lover look at her
And then turn suddenly to me.

title: The Rose And The Bee
content:

title: The Rose
content:

Pierrot was singing, singing;
I heard his lute the whole night thru
Until the east was red.

title: Fault
content:

They named them over one by one;
I laughed aloud when they were done,
I knew them all so well before,

title: Primavera Mia
content:
As kings who see their little life-day pass,
Take off the heavy ermine and the crown,
So had the trees that autumn-time laid down

title: Gray Eyes
content:
It was April when you came
The first time to me,
And my first look in your eyes
Was like my first look at the sea.

We have been together

title: But Not To Me
content:
The April night is still and sweet
With flowers on every tree;
Peace comes to them on quiet feet,
But not to me.

My peace is hidden in his breast

title: Only In Sleep
content:

Children I played with when I was a child,
Louise comes back with her brown hair braided,

title: Madeira From The Sea

content:
